data.title: FirstSun Capital Bancorp (FSUN) Net Interest Margin Holds Above 4% Challenging Bearish Narratives
data.description:
FirstSun Capital Bancorp (FSUN) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$101.7 million and basic EPS of US$0.77, alongside net income of US$21.6 million as investors weigh what these numbers mean against its recent growth record. Over the past year, the company has seen trailing twelve month revenue move from US$359.2 million to US$404.0 million, while EPS over the same period went from US$2.76 to US$3.44 as net income rose from US$75.6 million to US$96.0 million. With net profit margins holding in the mid 20% range, the latest quarter keeps the spotlight on how sustainably FirstSun is converting its revenue base into earnings.

See our full analysis for FirstSun Capital Bancorp.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up with the widely followed narratives around FirstSun's growth, quality, and risk profile, and where the numbers push back against those stories.

See what the community is saying about FirstSun Capital Bancorp

Advertisement

Net interest margin holds above 4%

  • FirstSun reported a Q1 2026 net interest margin of 4.19%, compared with 4.16% on a trailing twelve month basis. This keeps its core lending spread in a tight 4.1% to 4.2% range across the last several quarters where data is available.
  • Analysts' consensus view highlights relationship banking and fee-based services as key profit drivers. These margin levels sit alongside fee income that the narrative notes is above 25% of revenue, which together support:
    • The idea that earnings are not purely reliant on loan spreads, with the last twelve months producing US$95.95 million of net income at a 23.8% net margin.
    • The claim that operational efficiency can scale over time, as the cost to income ratio in the available trailing data sits close to 64%, suggesting room for operating leverage if revenue grows as forecast.

Asset quality steady with US$59.7 million in non performing loans

  • Non performing loans were US$59.66 million at Q1 2026, compared with US$60.77 million at Q4 2025 and US$78.59 million at Q1 2025, while total loans where disclosed held around US$6.5 billion to US$6.7 billion across the last year.
  • Critics highlight the bearish narrative concern about credit costs in areas like C&I and public finance, and the current figures interact with that view in a few ways:
    • The presence of US$59.66 million of non performing loans shows credit risk is real, yet the bank still produced US$95.95 million of trailing net income and a 23.8% net margin, so problem loans have not erased profitability in the period shown.
    • At the same time, the bearish point that asset quality can be volatile is reflected in the movement from US$78.59 million of non performing loans in early 2025 to the current level, which investors may watch alongside future charge off disclosures.
On the back of these credit trends, some investors look more closely at where the weak spots sit in the book before leaning into any growth story, and the bear case focuses heavily on how future charge offs might bite if conditions turn. It can help to see how that stacks up against the fuller narrative 🐻 FirstSun Capital Bancorp Bear Case

Growth and valuation gap stand out

Analysts in the dataset expect revenue to grow 34.4% per year and earnings 53.5% per year, against a current share price of US$37.31, an analyst target of US$45.33 and a DCF fair value of US$65.89, with the stock on a 10.9x P/E compared with 12.6x for peers and 11.5x for the US banks industry.

    • On one hand, the 16.3% five year earnings growth and 23.8% trailing net margin line up with revenue growth forecasts that sit well above the 11.1% US market forecast, which heavily supports the bullish case for stronger profit compounding than the broader market.
    • On the other, the step down to 10.4% earnings growth in the latest year shows growth has not matched the multi year pace recently, so investors weighing the bullish view might compare that cooling trend with the implied upside between US$37.31 and both the US$45.33 analyst target and US$65.89 DCF fair value.

data.title: Embla Medical Hf (CPSE:EMBLA) Net Income Drop Tests Bullish Growth Narrative
data.description:
Embla Medical hf (CPSE:EMBLA) has opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$232.4 million and basic EPS of US$0.034, setting the tone for how its earnings story is evolving against a current share price of DKK26.9. Over the past year, the company has seen trailing twelve month revenue move from US$854.9 million to US$958.3 million while basic EPS on the same basis went from US$0.160 to US$0.202. This gives investors a clearer view of how the top and bottom lines are tracking into this latest quarter. With a trailing net margin of 9% versus 8% a year earlier, the focus now is on how consistently Embla can convert growing sales into profit in the quarters ahead.

See our full analysis for Embla Medical hf.

With the headline numbers set, the next step is to see how these results line up with the dominant market narratives around Embla Medical hf's growth, profitability, and long term earnings power.

See what the community is saying about Embla Medical hf

Advertisement

Trailing Growth Holds Above 20%

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, revenue is US$958.3 million and net income is US$85.6 million, compared with US$854.9 million and US$68.3 million a year earlier, which lines up with the 22.1% earnings growth figure cited in the analysis data.
  • Bulls point to new product launches and broader reimbursement as drivers of faster future growth, and these trailing numbers give them some support, but also set a higher bar:
    • The bullish view talks about revenue growing around 9.7% per year and margins rising from about 8.4% to 10.6%, while the latest net margin is 9% on US$958.3 million of revenue. This means a good part of that margin improvement is already embedded in the starting point.
    • Consensus expects earnings to reach about US$112.8 million by 2028, compared with the current trailing US$85.6 million, so the bullish case effectively leans on Embla keeping growth close to recent levels for several more years.

Bulls argue this run rate could be the base case rather than the peak if product adoption and reimbursement keep supporting higher patient volumes, and that is the core of the optimistic narrative for the next few years. 🐂 Embla Medical hf Bull Case

Margins Steady Around 9%

  • Net margin on a trailing basis sits at 9%, up from 8% a year ago in the analysis data, while quarterly net income over the last five reported quarters has ranged between US$12.2 million and US$25.9 million on quarterly revenue between US$202.8 million and US$256.7 million.
  • Bears highlight rising compliance costs and pricing pressure as long term margin headwinds, and these figures give a useful check on that concern:
    • The cautious narrative expects margins to move from about 9.0% to 9.5% over the next three years, which is only a small step up from the current 9% outcome. Even the bearish case still embeds some margin stability rather than a clear squeeze.
    • At the same time, quarterly net income fell from US$25.9 million in Q3 2025 to US$14.5 million in Q1 2026 on broadly similar revenue levels, which shows how higher costs or pricing pressure in any given period can offset sales and supports the bears’ focus on expense and reimbursement risk.

Skeptics warn that if regulatory and pricing trends lean against the company, swings like the move from US$25.9 million to US$14.5 million can matter more than the smoother 9% trailing margin suggests. 🐻 Embla Medical hf Bear Case

P/E Of 21.4x Versus 33.60 Target

  • Embla trades on a trailing P/E of 21.4x, below the European Medical Equipment industry average of 22.8x and the peer average of 63.3x, while the analysis cites a DCF fair value of DKK30.11 and an analyst price target of DKK33.60 compared with the current share price of DKK26.9.
  • What stands out in the balanced narrative is how these valuations line up with the growth outlook:
    • Revenue is forecast to grow about 8% per year and earnings about 11.8% per year in the analysis. The fact the DCF fair value of DKK30.11 and the DKK33.60 target both sit above today’s DKK26.9 price suggests the market is not fully paying up for those forecasts.
    • At the same time, the consensus view also describes the stock as fairly priced around the target. This means any reassessment of growth or margins could move that target closer to or further from the current P/E level quite quickly.

data.title: Bloom Energy’s Record Quarter And Oracle AI Deal Reshape Growth Outlook
data.description:
• Bloom Energy (NYSE:BE) reported record-breaking Q1 revenue, gross margin, and operating income, surpassing its prior guidance.
  • The company significantly raised its full-year 2026 revenue outlook following these results.
  • Bloom Energy became the sole power provider for Oracle's Project Jupiter, an AI data center powered entirely by its fuel cell microgrids.

Bloom Energy enters this news cycle with its share price at $226.37 and very large 1-year and multi-year returns, including a 129.4% gain year to date and 69.9% over the past month. For readers following NYSE:BE, this move comes after a 2.5% rise over the past week, which suggests heightened attention around the latest company updates.

What stands out here is the combination of record quarterly metrics, a higher 2026 revenue outlook, and a flagship AI data center deployment with Oracle. For investors watching the buildout of AI infrastructure, Bloom Energy is presenting its fuel cell technology as a power option that can support large-scale, rapidly deployable compute facilities.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Bloom Energy by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Bloom Energy.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Bloom Energy following this latest news.

For existing and prospective investors, this update is about both financial delivery and proof of concept. Bloom Energy moved from a net loss of US$23.81 million in Q1 last year to net income of US$70.65 million on revenue of US$751.05 million, and raised its 2026 revenue outlook to US$3.4b to US$3.8b. That shift to profitability, combined with record top line and margins, suggests the business is now converting its large order book into earnings rather than just headline contracts. The Oracle Project Jupiter deal, which replaces planned gas turbines and diesel with up to 2.45 gigawatts of Bloom fuel cells in a single microgrid, also sends a strong signal to the market that fuel cells can sit at the heart of large AI data centers, not just on the fringes as backup power.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Bloom Energy Narrative

  • The record quarter and raised 2026 guidance align with the narrative that growing AI data center power demand and hyperscaler partnerships can support higher revenue and improved earnings visibility.
  • The size of the Oracle deployment raises the execution bar on manufacturing expansion and project delivery. The narrative already flags this as a key operational risk if demand timing or project schedules change.
  • The specific choice to replace turbines and diesel with a large fuel cell microgrid, with environmental benefits around emissions and water use, adds detail on customer adoption drivers that is only partly reflected in the broader narrative.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Bloom Energy to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have highlighted three company risk factors, including a volatile share price over the past 3 months, recent shareholder dilution, and substantial insider selling. Together these point to sentiment and governance signals that are important to watch as the stock reacts to this news.
  • ⚠️ Bloom Energy still relies on natural gas feedstock and faces competition from zero emissions options such as solar, wind, and battery storage, as well as fuel cell and clean power peers like Plug Power and NextEra affiliated projects, which could influence long term demand and pricing power.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow at a strong rate, and the Q1 move into profitability provides evidence that higher volumes from large data center contracts, including Oracle and other hyperscalers, can translate into positive net income rather than just revenue growth.
  • 🎁 Project Jupiter positions Bloom alongside large data center and cloud providers such as Oracle and other hyperscaler customers, reinforcing its role in AI infrastructure power solutions at a time when many investors are looking for listed ways to gain exposure to that theme.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, the key questions are whether Bloom Energy can repeat Q1 level revenue and margins, and how quickly Oracle and other AI data center customers turn multi gigawatt agreements into installed and revenue generating capacity. Investors may also want to track any further changes to 2026 guidance, updates on manufacturing capacity additions, and signs that contract wins are broadening beyond early hyperscaler adopters. Price action, insider trading activity, and any changes in risk flags could give additional clues on how the market is reassessing the balance between rapid growth, capital intensity, and competitive threats in data center power.

To stay informed on how the latest news impacts the investment narrative for Bloom Energy, head to the community page for Bloom Energy to keep up with the top community narratives.

At a P/E of 36.1x, BGC Group trades at a higher earnings multiple than its direct peer group average of 18.4x, even though it sits below the broader US Capital Markets industry average of 42.1x.

The P/E ratio compares the share price to earnings per share and is often used for capital markets and brokerage firms where earnings are a key focus. A higher P/E can indicate that investors are willing to pay more today for each dollar of current earnings. This usually reflects expectations for stronger profit growth or a higher quality earnings stream.

data.title: Atea (OB:ATEA) Margin Improvement Reinforces Bullish Earnings Narrative
data.description:
Atea (OB:ATEA) has opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of NOK 9.7b and basic EPS of NOK 3.5, while the latest trailing twelve month run rate sits at NOK 38.5b of revenue and NOK 10.0 in basic EPS. Over recent quarters the company has seen quarterly revenue move between NOK 8.4b and NOK 11.3b with basic EPS ranging from NOK 1.41 to NOK 3.5, and the trailing twelve month revenue series progressing from NOK 34.6b to NOK 38.5b alongside EPS moving from NOK 6.8 to NOK 10.0. For investors, the picture is one of earnings power that is being backed by widening net profit margins, giving this set of results a more robust quality.

See our full analysis for Atea.

With the latest numbers on the table, the next step is to see how they line up with the most widely held stories about Atea and where those narratives may need to be updated.

See what the community is saying about Atea

Advertisement

TTM earnings climb to NOK 1.1b

  • Over the last 12 months, Atea generated NOK 1.1b of net income and NOK 9.97 in basic EPS on NOK 38.5b of revenue, compared with NOK 878m of net income and NOK 7.91 EPS on NOK 37.4b of revenue one year earlier.
  • Bulls point to this 48.3% trailing earnings growth and 2.9% net margin as evidence that Atea can support higher long term earnings power, yet:
    • Forecast earnings growth of about 3.7% per year is much closer to the 5 year average of 3% than to the latest 48.3% jump, so the bullish view leans heavily on recent strength continuing.
    • Revenue is forecast to grow around 5.8% per year, which is faster than the 2.6% cited for the Norwegian market, and that gap is a key part of the bullish case that Atea can keep outgrowing its home market.
Over the past year Atea delivered a sharp step up in profitability that bullish investors see as the new baseline rather than a one off, and they argue that continued margin gains and above market revenue growth could keep earnings on a firmer track than the forecasts imply. 🐂 Atea Bull Case

Improving 2.9% margin vs 2.1%

  • The trailing net profit margin of 2.9% compares with 2.1% a year earlier, on revenue rising from NOK 34.6b to NOK 38.5b and EPS moving from NOK 6.85 to NOK 9.97 over that span.
  • Bears focus on structural pressure from cloud and software margin changes, and this margin picture gives both support and pushback to that view:
    • Critics highlight that vendor incentive changes in software and services and the shift toward cloud could weigh on future margins, yet the most recent 12 month data still shows a higher net margin than last year.
    • At the same time, forecast earnings growth of 3.7% per year is below the broader Norwegian market’s 11.2% forecast, which bears use to argue that even with better margins Atea may grow more slowly than many domestic alternatives.
Skeptics warn that margin gains could be hard to hold if hardware mix and vendor incentives move against Atea, while the recent 2.9% margin and higher EPS show that, so far, those headwinds are not overwhelming the business. 🐻 Atea Bear Case

Valuation sits below DCF fair value

  • At a share price of NOK 154.20 and a P/E of 15.5x, Atea trades below the cited DCF fair value of NOK 289.21 and below the European IT industry and peer P/E averages of 18.8x and 18.5x, while offering a 4.86% dividend yield.
  • Consensus narrative frames this as potential value supported by improving fundamentals, but there are some tensions to weigh:
    • On one hand, trailing earnings growth of 48.3% and the higher 2.9% net margin help explain why some investors see a gap between price and DCF fair value at NOK 289.21.
    • On the other hand, the 3.7% forecast earnings growth rate is below the Norwegian market’s 11.2%, so the lower P/E multiple may partly reflect expectations for slower earnings growth than the wider market despite the current income yield of 4.86%.

Next Steps
